text: Last class I took the subway alone and asked a passenger for help.
Today I want to ask for help if I feel sick in another country.
Pharmacy
Fever
Headache
Sore throat
Medicine
Painkiller
Symptom
Pharmacist
Emergency
Dizzy
If I have a fever, I will rest at the hotel.
If the medicine does not help, I will see a doctor.
In an emergency, you must call one, one, two.
You have to stay calm and speak slowly.
Excuse me. I have a fever and a sore throat. What do you recommend?
I am sorry to hear that. This painkiller will help. If you take one tablet every six hours, your fever will go down.
Thank you. Do I have to take it before or after meals?
After meals, with water. You must not take more than four a day. And you must not drink alcohol with it.
Understood. And if I still feel sick tomorrow?
If the fever does not go down, you have to see a doctor. There is a clinic near the central station. Rest and drink water. You will feel better soon!
I have a fever and a sore throat.
Where is the nearest pharmacy?
Do I have to take it after meals?
If it gets worse, I will see a doctor.
This is an emergency. Please call for help.

Attention, all guests. This is a safety message from the hotel front desk. Please read this carefully before you go out today. If you feel sick during your stay, do not worry. You can call the front desk at any time, and we will help you find a doctor. There is a small pharmacy two blocks from the hotel, on the main avenue. It opens at eight in the morning and closes at ten at night. If your problem is serious, you must call the emergency number, which is one, one, two. Keep this number with you. When you call, you have to stay calm and speak slowly. Tell them where you are and what the problem is. If you take any medicine every day, you should carry it with you and keep the box. And remember, if you get lost or you feel unsafe, you can always come back to the hotel. We are here to help you. Have a safe and happy trip.

Hi Tuca, it is your friend again. I heard you were not feeling well this morning, so I wanted to give you some advice. First, you must rest today. Do not walk too much, okay? If you still have a headache tonight, you should go to the pharmacy on the avenue and ask for some medicine. The pharmacist speaks a little English, so just say you have a fever and a sore throat. If the medicine does not help by tomorrow, you have to see a doctor. There is a clinic near the central station, and it is not expensive. Oh, and you must drink a lot of water. If you drink water and rest, you will feel much better soon. Call me if you need anything. I can go with you to the clinic if you want. Take care of yourself. Bye.

First, Tuca woke up with a headache and a sore throat. Then she called the front desk and asked for help. After that, she walked to the pharmacy on the avenue. Next, she described her symptoms and bought some medicine. Finally, she went back to the hotel to rest and drink water.
